notes: Level 4 manual review override shard for spell indexes 20-39 in generated discovery row order.

freedom-of-movement::terrain_or_surface.issue: The target ignores Difficult Terrain for movement, gains a Swim Speed equal to its Speed, and can spend 5 feet of movement to escape nonmagical restraints; runtime stores these movement rules only in prose.
freedom-of-movement::terrain_or_surface.recommendedTemplateChange: Add movement immunity/adaptation fields for difficult-terrain immunity, swim speed equal to walking speed, and movement-cost escape from restraints.

freedom-of-movement::status_or_state_cha.issue: Magical effects cannot reduce the target's Speed or inflict Paralyzed or Restrained, and the target can automatically escape nonmagical restraints including Grappled for 5 feet of movement; runtime lacks typed prevention and escape mechanics.
freedom-of-movement::status_or_state_cha.recommendedTemplateChange: Add condition/speed prevention fields and nonmagical restraint auto-escape fields.
freedom-of-movement::status_or_state_cha.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime prevention and restraintEscape data.

freedom-of-movement::environmental_chang.issue: The detector matched Difficult Terrain, but the spell changes the target's movement interaction rather than changing the environment.

giant-insect::choice_or_mode.issue: The caster chooses centipede, spider, or wasp, and the form changes stat-block details; runtime lacks typed form options.
giant-insect::choice_or_mode.recommendedTemplateChange: Add summon form option fields with form-specific abilities.
giant-insect::choice_or_mode.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime summon.formOptions data for centipede/spider/wasp.

giant-insect::attack_or_save_modifier.issue: The stat block includes spell-attack-based attacks, a spell-save-DC Constitution save, Spider Climb with no ability check, and level-derived multiattack; runtime only has prose.
giant-insect::attack_or_save_modifier.recommendedTemplateChange: Add summoned stat-block action fields for attack bonus source, save DC source, form-specific actions, and level-derived multiattack.
giant-insect::attack_or_save_modifier.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime summon statBlock actions and scaling.

giant-insect::target_filter_or_eligibili.issue: The insect manifests in an unoccupied space the caster can see within range; runtime has point targeting but no typed unoccupied-space placement rule.
giant-insect::target_filter_or_eligibili.recommendedTemplateChange: Add summon placement eligibility fields for unoccupied visible space.

giant-insect::terrain_or_surface.issue: Spider Climb lets the insect climb difficult surfaces and ceilings without an ability check; runtime lacks form-specific movement traits.
giant-insect::terrain_or_surface.recommendedTemplateChange: Add summoned creature movement trait fields for spider climb and form-specific speeds.

giant-insect::vision_light_sound.issue: The detector matched visible placement and a target the insect can see for an action; these are targeting/placement filters, not light, vision, or sound effects.
giant-insect::vision_light_sound.recommendedTemplateChange: Track under summon placement and action target filters.

giant-insect::message_or_communication.issue: The insect understands the languages the caster knows; runtime lacks typed summoned-language fields.

giant-insect::summon_or_controlled_entit.issue: The spell summons an allied creature with initiative timing, no-action verbal commands, default Dodge and danger avoidance, form options, scaled stats, and form-specific actions; runtime is still a generic utility effect.
giant-insect::summon_or_controlled_entit.recommendedTemplateChange: Add full summoned entity fields for allegiance, initiative, command economy, default behavior, form options, stats, and actions.
giant-insect::summon_or_controlled_entit.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime SUMMONING or summonEntity data for Giant Insect.

giant-insect::social_or_knowledge_effect.issue: The insect is allied to the caster and allies and understands the caster's languages; runtime lacks typed allegiance and language inheritance.
giant-insect::social_or_knowledge_effect.recommendedTemplateChange: Add summoned allegiance and language fields.
giant-insect::social_or_knowledge_effect.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime summon.allegiance and summon.languages data.

giant-insect::object_stats_or_damageabil.issue: The stat block has AC, HP, speed, senses, attacks, save actions, and spell-level scaling; runtime lacks typed summon stats.
giant-insect::object_stats_or_damageabil.recommendedTemplateChange: Add summoned stat-block fields with spell-level formulas.

giant-insect::conditional_ending.issue: The creature disappears at 0 Hit Points or when the spell ends; runtime lacks summon disappearance conditions.
giant-insect::conditional_ending.recommendedTemplateChange: Add summoned entity disappearance fields for at-zero-HP and spell-end.
giant-insect::conditional_ending.closedReason: Reused the existing created_entity_drops_to_0_hp conditional-ending trigger, recorded it in the structured spell fields, and mapped it to the runtime utility effect with effect scope for the summoned creature disappearance.

giant-insect::aftermath_or_memory.issue: The creature disappearing at 0 HP or spell end leaves no persistent aftermath or memory state in the canonical prose.
giant-insect::aftermath_or_memory.recommendedTemplateChange: Track under summon disappearance rather than aftermath.

grasping-vine::attack_or_save_modifier.issue: The caster can repeat the vine's melee spell attack as a Bonus Action on later turns against a creature within 30 feet of the vine; runtime has the initial attack but no granted repeat-attack action.
grasping-vine::attack_or_save_modifier.recommendedTemplateChange: Add repeat spell-attack action fields for created-object attacks using a secondary origin.
grasping-vine::attack_or_save_modifier.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime granted/repeatAttack data for the vine.

grasping-vine::target_filter_or_eligibil.issue: The vine sprouts from a surface in an unoccupied visible space; runtime targets a point but lacks surface and unoccupied-space placement rules.
grasping-vine::target_filter_or_eligibil.recommendedTemplateChange: Add created hazard/object placement fields for surface requirement and unoccupied visible space.

grasping-vine::sustain_or_recast_action.issue: The Bonus Action repeat attack is a sustained spell action from the vine's position; runtime lacks repeat action semantics.
grasping-vine::sustain_or_recast_action.recommendedTemplateChange: Add sustained/repeat attack fields for bonus-action repeats from created entities.

grasping-vine::forced_movement.issue: On a hit, the target is pulled up to 30 feet toward the vine; runtime now has a MOVEMENT pull effect.
grasping-vine::forced_movement.recommendedTemplateChange: No new forced-movement field required for this pass.
grasping-vine::forced_movement.recommendedJsonChange: Closed by adding a MOVEMENT pull effect.

grasping-vine::terrain_or_surface.issue: The vine must sprout from a surface in an unoccupied visible space, but runtime lacks surface placement semantics.
grasping-vine::terrain_or_surface.recommendedTemplateChange: Add surface placement fields for created vines/objects.

grasping-vine::vision_light_sound.issue: The detector matched ordinary visible placement, which is line-of-sight targeting rather than a light, vision, or sound mechanic.

grasping-vine::ward_alarm_or_trigger.issue: The detector matched hit-triggered pull and Grappled effects; these are attack result mechanics rather than ward/alarm triggers.
grasping-vine::ward_alarm_or_trigger.recommendedTemplateChange: Track under attack result, movement, and status fields.
grasping-vine::ward_alarm_or_trigger.recommendedJsonChange: Closed by DAMAGE, MOVEMENT, and STATUS_CONDITION effects.

grasping-vine::status_or_state_change.issue: Grappled is typed with the correct spell-save escape DC, but the Huge-or-smaller eligibility, one-creature grapple limit, free release, and higher-level grapple capacity are not first-class.
grasping-vine::status_or_state_change.recommendedTemplateChange: Add grapple rider fields for size limit, capacity, free release, and capacity scaling.

grasping-vine::conditional_ending.issue: The Grappled condition has the canonical escape DC and otherwise follows duration/concentration; no separate conditional ending is needed.
grasping-vine::conditional_ending.recommendedJsonChange: Closed by corrected escapeCheck and duration.

greater-invisibility::vision_light_sound.issue: The spell grants the Invisible condition, which is typed as status but also affects visibility/being seen; runtime has no explicit visibility-state semantics beyond the condition name.
greater-invisibility::vision_light_sound.recommendedTemplateChange: Add condition semantics mapping for Invisible or a visibility-state field if runtime needs it outside status name lookup.
greater-invisibility::vision_light_sound.recommendedJsonChange: Add/confirm runtime condition semantics for Invisible.

greater-invisibility::status_or_state_ch.issue: Invisible is typed, and fake escape/repeat save data was removed.
greater-invisibility::status_or_state_ch.recommendedTemplateChange: No new status field required for this pass.
greater-invisibility::status_or_state_ch.recommendedJsonChange: Closed by existing STATUS_CONDITION effect after removing fake escape/repeat fields.

greater-invisibility::conditional_ending.issue: The Invisible condition lasts until the normal spell duration ends; there is no special conditional ending.

guardian-of-faith::attack_or_save_modifi.issue: The Dexterity save and half-damage outcome are represented, and runtime now splits the canonical movement-entry and turn-start damage triggers.
guardian-of-faith::attack_or_save_modifi.recommendedTemplateChange: No new attack/save field required for this pass.
guardian-of-faith::attack_or_save_modifi.recommendedJsonChange: Closed by using on_enter_area and turn_start DAMAGE effects.

guardian-of-faith::target_filter_or_elig.issue: The guardian appears in an unoccupied visible space, occupies that space, only damages enemies within 10 feet, is invulnerable, and vanishes after dealing 60 total damage; runtime still lacks typed created-guardian placement, enemy-only aura filtering, invulnerability, and total damage pool semantics.
guardian-of-faith::target_filter_or_elig.recommendedTemplateChange: Add created guardian/hazard placement, enemy-only trigger filter, invulnerability, occupied-space, and total damage budget fields.
guardian-of-faith::target_filter_or_elig.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime createdGuardian or auraHazard data for placement, enemy filtering, invulnerability, occupied space, and 60-damage depletion.

guardian-of-faith::movement_or_repositio.issue: The detector matched hover in the appearance sentence, but the guardian does not move or reposition after placement.
guardian-of-faith::movement_or_repositio.recommendedTemplateChange: Track initial placement under created guardian/hazard placement fields rather than movement.

guardian-of-faith::vision_light_sound.issue: The detector matched ordinary visible placement and deity-appropriate appearance; there is no light, vision, or sound mechanic.

guardian-of-faith::deferred_descriptive_.issue: The deity-appropriate spectral form is descriptive flavor; placement, invulnerability, enemy aura damage, and depletion are tracked in mechanical buckets instead.
guardian-of-faith::deferred_descriptive_.recommendedTemplateChange: Keep appearance prose deferred unless visual display fields are later desired.
guardian-of-faith::deferred_descriptive_.recommendedJsonChange: No runtime data required for the deity-appropriate visual form.

guardian-of-nature::choice_or_mode.issue: The canonical snapshot names Primal Beast and Great Tree but omits the actual bullet benefits, so the mode mechanic cannot be safely encoded from this source.
guardian-of-nature::choice_or_mode.recommendedTemplateChange: Backfill trustworthy canonical prose for this spell before closing mode fields.
guardian-of-nature::choice_or_mode.recommendedJsonChange: Do not infer Primal Beast or Great Tree mechanics until the missing prose is restored.

guardian-of-nature::terrain_or_surface.issue: The only terrain signal is a referenced Difficult Terrain tooltip without the omitted Great Tree benefit text, so the actual terrain rule is unavailable in the canonical snapshot.
guardian-of-nature::terrain_or_surface.recommendedTemplateChange: Backfill trustworthy canonical prose before adding terrain fields.
guardian-of-nature::terrain_or_surface.recommendedJsonChange: Do not add terrain runtime data from tooltip remnants alone.

guardian-of-nature::summon_or_controlled.issue: The nature spirit wording describes the transformation source and does not create or control a separate summoned entity.
guardian-of-nature::summon_or_controlled.recommendedTemplateChange: Track the real mechanics under transformation/mode fields once the missing benefits are backfilled.

guardian-of-nature::healing_or_restorati.issue: The referenced temporary hit points tooltip suggests an omitted benefit, but the canonical snapshot lacks the amount and mode relationship.
guardian-of-nature::healing_or_restorati.recommendedTemplateChange: Backfill the missing benefits before adding temporary-hit-point fields.
guardian-of-nature::healing_or_restorati.recommendedJsonChange: Do not add temporary-hit-point runtime data from tooltip remnants alone.

guardian-of-nature::object_stats_or_dama.issue: The detector matched the words Hit Points in the temporary hit points tooltip; this is not object durability or damageability.
guardian-of-nature::object_stats_or_dama.recommendedTemplateChange: No object-stat field required for this finding.

guardian-of-nature::environmental_change.issue: The canonical snapshot omits the Great Tree benefits, so any environmental or enemy-terrain mechanic cannot be safely separated from flavor.
guardian-of-nature::environmental_change.recommendedTemplateChange: Backfill trustworthy canonical prose before adding environmental fields.
guardian-of-nature::environmental_change.recommendedJsonChange: Do not add environmental runtime data until the benefit text is restored.

guardian-of-nature::conditional_ending.issue: The transformation lasts until the spell ends and has no separate conditional ending beyond duration and concentration.

hallucinatory-terrain::terrain_or_surfac.issue: The spell changes how natural terrain looks, sounds, and smells but does not change the real tactile terrain or movement rules.
hallucinatory-terrain::terrain_or_surfac.recommendedTemplateChange: Track under illusion/disbelief rather than real terrain.

hallucinatory-terrain::vision_light_soun.issue: The sensory illusion is preserved in the UTILITY description and runtime utilityType sensory; the missing mechanical part is disbelief, tracked under illusion/disguise.
hallucinatory-terrain::vision_light_soun.recommendedTemplateChange: No separate vision-light-sound field required for this pass.
hallucinatory-terrain::vision_light_soun.recommendedJsonChange: No separate vision-light-sound runtime data required.

hallucinatory-terrain::illusion_or_disgu.issue: The illusion has tactile unchanged, likely-notice-on-entry, Study action, Intelligence (Investigation) check against spell save DC, and vague-superimposed-image-on-success rules; runtime preserves them only as prose and flattens structured Utility Type illusion into sensory.
hallucinatory-terrain::illusion_or_disgu.recommendedTemplateChange: Add illusion/disbelief fields for affected senses, unchanged senses, examination action, check ability/skill/DC, and success perception.
hallucinatory-terrain::illusion_or_disgu.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime illusion/disbelief data; keep utilityType sensory only as a temporary enum-compatible value until illusion is supported.

hallucinatory-terrain::created_object_or.issue: The prose says manufactured structures, equipment, and creatures are not changed; it does not create objects or structures.

hallucinatory-terrain::social_or_knowled.issue: The Intelligence (Investigation) Study action is a disbelief mechanic for the illusion, not a separate social or knowledge effect.
hallucinatory-terrain::social_or_knowled.recommendedJsonChange: No separate social/knowledge runtime data required.

hallucinatory-terrain::environmental_cha.issue: The real environment is unchanged; only sensory appearance, sound, and smell are altered.
hallucinatory-terrain::environmental_cha.recommendedTemplateChange: Track under illusion/disbelief rather than environmental change.

ice-storm::attack_or_save_modifier.issue: The Dexterity save and half-damage outcome are represented for both damage components.

ice-storm::terrain_or_surface.issue: The ground in the Cylinder becoming Difficult Terrain until the end of the caster's next turn is now represented.
ice-storm::terrain_or_surface.recommendedTemplateChange: Closed by adding TERRAIN to structured Effect Type.
ice-storm::terrain_or_surface.recommendedJsonChange: Closed by adding a TERRAIN difficult effect with Cylinder area and one-round duration.

ice-storm::environmental_change.issue: The environmental consequence is specifically difficult terrain on the ground and is now tracked under TERRAIN.

leomunds-secret-chest::target_filter_or_.issue: The recalled chest appears in an unoccupied space on the ground within 5 feet of the caster; runtime preserves this only in prose.
leomunds-secret-chest::target_filter_or_.recommendedTemplateChange: Add object recall/placement fields for unoccupied ground space and distance from caster.
leomunds-secret-chest::target_filter_or_.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime objectPlacement or recallPlacement data.

leomunds-secret-chest::terrain_or_surfac.issue: The recalled chest must appear on the ground, but runtime lacks a typed ground-surface placement rule.
leomunds-secret-chest::terrain_or_surfac.recommendedTemplateChange: Add placement surface fields for returned objects.
leomunds-secret-chest::terrain_or_surfac.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime placement.surface = ground for the recall action.

leomunds-secret-chest::travel_or_planar_.issue: The chest and contents move to the Ethereal Plane, can be recalled to the caster, can be sent back, and may remain findable there if the spell ends; runtime stores this planar object movement only in prose.
leomunds-secret-chest::travel_or_planar_.recommendedTemplateChange: Add object planar-storage fields for destination plane, recall action, send-back action, return placement, and end-state.

leomunds-secret-chest::social_or_knowled.issue: The possibility that someone else can find the chest is an aftermath/end-state consequence of planar storage, not a social or knowledge effect.
leomunds-secret-chest::social_or_knowled.recommendedTemplateChange: Track under planar storage and conditional ending.
leomunds-secret-chest::social_or_knowled.recommendedJsonChange: No separate social/knowledge runtime data required.

leomunds-secret-chest::object_stats_or_d.issue: Destroying the Tiny replica chest ends the spell, but runtime lacks typed dependency/destruction semantics for the material replica.
leomunds-secret-chest::object_stats_or_d.recommendedTemplateChange: Add component-dependency fields for destroyable linked objects that end a spell.
leomunds-secret-chest::object_stats_or_d.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime linkedComponentDependency data for the replica chest.

leomunds-secret-chest::conditional_endin.issue: The spell has a cumulative 5 percent daily ending chance after 60 days, ends if cast again, and ends if the Tiny replica is destroyed; runtime preserves these only in prose.
leomunds-secret-chest::conditional_endin.recommendedTemplateChange: Add conditional ending fields for delayed cumulative chance, recast ending, and linked-object destruction.
leomunds-secret-chest::conditional_endin.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime conditionalEnding data for the 60-day cumulative chance, recast ending, and replica destruction.

leomunds-secret-chest::deferred_descript.closedReason: Closed as a deferred-flavor misbucket only. The same planar object-storage mechanics remain actionable under leomunds-secret-chest::travel_or_planar_movement, with placement and ending details tracked by the adjacent target_filter_or_eligibility, terrain_or_surface, object_stats_or_damageability, and conditional_ending findings.
leomunds-secret-chest::deferred_descript.issue: The detector put Ethereal Plane storage and recall prose into the flavor bucket, but these are mechanical planar object-storage rules rather than purely descriptive flavor.
leomunds-secret-chest::deferred_descript.recommendedTemplateChange: Treat planar storage/recall as a mechanical bucket, not deferred flavor.
leomunds-secret-chest::deferred_descript.recommendedJsonChange: Move this behavior into typed planar object-storage runtime data when that field family exists.

locate-creature::vision_light_sound.issue: The detector matched the prerequisite that the caster has seen a kind of creature up close; this is familiarity/targeting history, not a vision, light, or sound effect.
locate-creature::vision_light_sound.recommendedTemplateChange: Track under locate-query and target eligibility fields.

locate-creature::social_or_knowledge_eff.issue: The spell has a typed information query: specific familiar creature or nearest creature of a seen-up-close kind, 1,000-foot sensing range, direction to location and movement, different-form failure, and lead-path blocking; runtime stores all of this only in prose.
locate-creature::social_or_knowledge_eff.recommendedTemplateChange: Add locate/detection fields for query mode, familiarity prerequisite, sensing range, returned information, blocker materials, and form-change failure.

mordenkainens-faithful-hound::attack_or_.issue: The hound's Dexterity save now triggers at the start of each caster turn and deals no damage on a successful save.
mordenkainens-faithful-hound::attack_or_.recommendedTemplateChange: No attack/save field change required for this pass.
mordenkainens-faithful-hound::attack_or_.recommendedJsonChange: Closed by changing the DAMAGE trigger to turn_start and saveEffect to negates_condition.

mordenkainens-faithful-hound::target_fil.issue: The hound is created in an unoccupied visible space, bites one enemy within 5 feet, and barks when a Small-or-larger creature comes within 30 feet without speaking the password; runtime lacks typed placement, enemy-only bite target, size filter, proximity trigger, and password exception fields.
mordenkainens-faithful-hound::target_fil.recommendedTemplateChange: Add created hound/ward fields for placement, enemy bite target, creature size trigger, proximity radius, and password exception.
mordenkainens-faithful-hound::target_fil.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime createdHound or wardGuardian data.

mordenkainens-faithful-hound::vision_lig.issue: Only the caster can see the hound, and the hound has Truesight 30 feet; runtime preserves these visibility and sense mechanics only in prose.
mordenkainens-faithful-hound::vision_lig.recommendedTemplateChange: Add created-entity visibility and senses fields.
mordenkainens-faithful-hound::vision_lig.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime hound visibility and senses data.

mordenkainens-faithful-hound::message_or.issue: The caster specifies a password, and the hound barks loudly when a qualifying creature approaches without speaking it; runtime lacks typed password and audible alarm data.
mordenkainens-faithful-hound::message_or.recommendedTemplateChange: Add ward/alarm password and audible alert fields.
mordenkainens-faithful-hound::message_or.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime alarmTrigger data for password-gated barking.

mordenkainens-private-sanctum::healing_o.issue: The detector matched the word secure; this spell does not heal or restore anything.

otilukes-resilient-sphere::attack_or_sav.issue: Only an unwilling creature makes a Dexterity save to avoid enclosure; runtime has a generic UTILITY effect with no unwilling-target save gate.
otilukes-resilient-sphere::attack_or_sav.recommendedTemplateChange: Add enclosure save fields for unwilling creatures while allowing willing creatures and objects without a save.
otilukes-resilient-sphere::attack_or_sav.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime enclosure save data or split effects for willing/object and unwilling-creature targets.

otilukes-resilient-sphere::forced_moveme.issue: An enclosed creature can spend an action to roll the sphere at up to half its Speed, and other creatures can pick up and move the globe; runtime stores this movement only in prose.
otilukes-resilient-sphere::forced_moveme.recommendedTemplateChange: Add enclosed-object movement fields for internal push action, half-speed cap, and external pickup/move.

otilukes-resilient-sphere::terrain_or_su.issue: The detector matched sphere walls, but these are barrier/object mechanics rather than terrain or surface changes.

otilukes-resilient-sphere::created_objec.issue: The spell creates a damage-immune enclosing barrier that blocks physical objects, energy, and spell effects in both directions while allowing breathing; runtime preserves barrier rules only in prose.
otilukes-resilient-sphere::created_objec.recommendedTemplateChange: Add barrier/enclosure fields for pass-through blocking, damage immunity, breathing exception, inside/outside damage prevention, and object/creature containment.

otilukes-resilient-sphere::deferred_desc.closedReason: Closed as a deferred-flavor misbucket only. The same enclosure mechanics remain actionable under otilukes-resilient-sphere::created_object_or_structure, attack_or_save_modifier, forced_movement, target_filter_or_eligibility, and conditional_ending; the damage-prevention slice is already handled by the damage_reduction_or_prevention bucket.
otilukes-resilient-sphere::deferred_desc.issue: The detector put the enclosing sphere into flavor, but the sphere is a mechanical barrier/enclosure with targeting, save, movement, damage immunity, and Disintegrate destruction rules.
otilukes-resilient-sphere::deferred_desc.recommendedTemplateChange: Split shimmering visual flavor from barrier/enclosure mechanics.
otilukes-resilient-sphere::deferred_desc.recommendedJsonChange: Move the mechanical barrier/enclosure behavior into typed runtime data.

phantasmal-killer::attack_or_save_modifi.issue: Runtime now represents initial and recurring Psychic damage saves, but the failed-save Disadvantage on ability checks and attack rolls is not fully modeled; attack-roll disadvantage can be represented, but ability-check disadvantage and tying both riders to the same initial half-damage save need a better shared-save rider model.
phantasmal-killer::attack_or_save_modifi.recommendedTemplateChange: Add disadvantage-on-ability-check fields and shared-save rider fields.
phantasmal-killer::attack_or_save_modifi.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime abilityCheckModifier data and avoid duplicate-save attack modifier effects until shared-save riders are supported.

phantasmal-killer::repeat_save_or_recurr.issue: The end-of-turn Wisdom save for recurring Psychic damage is now represented as a turn_end DAMAGE effect with save success negating the recurring damage.
phantasmal-killer::repeat_save_or_recurr.recommendedTemplateChange: Closed by adding a second scaling rule for the turn_end damage.
phantasmal-killer::repeat_save_or_recurr.recommendedJsonChange: Closed by adding a turn_end DAMAGE effect.

phantasmal-killer::vision_light_sound.issue: The fear illusion is visible only to the target; runtime preserves this visibility scope only in prose.

phantasmal-killer::illusion_or_disguise.issue: The spell creates a target-only fear illusion that drives damage and disadvantage, but runtime has no typed illusion payload beyond prose.
phantasmal-killer::illusion_or_disguise.recommendedTemplateChange: Add hostile illusion fields for visible-to-target-only, fear manifestation, and linked damage/rider effects.

phantasmal-killer::conditional_ending.issue: A successful initial save deals half damage and ends the spell, and a successful recurring save ends the spell; runtime can negate recurring damage but lacks typed spell-ending-on-save-success semantics.
phantasmal-killer::conditional_ending.recommendedTemplateChange: Closed by adding Conditional Ending Trigger values for initial_save_success and repeat_save_success.
phantasmal-killer::conditional_ending.recommendedJsonChange: Closed by adding runtime conditionalEndings to the initial and recurring damage effects.
phantasmal-killer::conditional_ending.reviewerNote: Closed for save-success spell-ending semantics. The failed-save disadvantage rider and hostile illusion payload remain open in their separate Phantasmal Killer rows.

polymorph::choice_or_mode.issue: The caster chooses a Beast form constrained by CR or target level, and that choice replaces most statistics while preserving alignment, personality, creature type, Hit Points, and Hit Point Dice; runtime keeps this transformation payload only in prose.
polymorph::choice_or_mode.recommendedTemplateChange: Add transformation form-choice fields for allowed creature type, CR/level cap, replaced stats, retained stats, and action restrictions.
polymorph::choice_or_mode.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime transformation data for Beast form selection and retained/replaced statistics.

polymorph::attack_or_save_modifier.issue: The Wisdom save gate is now represented on the transformation utility effect.
polymorph::attack_or_save_modifier.recommendedTemplateChange: No attack/save field change required for this pass.
polymorph::attack_or_save_modifier.recommendedJsonChange: Closed by changing the UTILITY condition to a Wisdom save with negates_condition.

polymorph::vision_light_sound.issue: The detector matched ordinary line-of-sight targeting; the spell has no light, vision, or sound effect.

polymorph::message_or_communication.issue: The target cannot speak or cast spells in the new form, but runtime lacks typed speech and spellcasting restriction fields.
polymorph::message_or_communication.recommendedTemplateChange: Add transformation restriction fields for speech, spellcasting, and anatomy-limited actions.

polymorph::healing_or_restoration.issue: The target gains Temporary Hit Points equal to the Beast form's Hit Points, and those points vanish at spell end; runtime lacks typed temporary-hit-point data tied to chosen form HP.
polymorph::healing_or_restoration.recommendedTemplateChange: Add temporary-hit-point formula fields that can reference chosen form statistics.

polymorph::object_stats_or_damageability.issue: The chosen Beast stat block replaces most game statistics while preserving listed identity/HP fields; runtime has no typed stat replacement map.

polymorph::conditional_ending.issue: Closed: Polymorph's early ending when the transformation Temporary Hit Points are depleted is now represented in structured markdown and runtime JSON.
polymorph::conditional_ending.recommendedTemplateChange: Closed by Conditional Ending Triggers temporary_hit_points_depleted with effect scope.
polymorph::conditional_ending.recommendedJsonChange: Closed by runtime conditionalEndings on the transformation utility effect.
polymorph::conditional_ending.closedReason: Polymorph now records the early ending trigger separately from the still-open temporary-hit-point formula/stat-replacement work. The Beast-form Temporary Hit Point amount remains tracked under healing_or_restoration and transformation buckets.

polymorph::aftermath_or_memory.issue: Temporary hit points vanishing at spell end is part of the transformation's normal cleanup, not a persistent aftermath or memory effect.

shadow-of-moil::vision_light_sound.issue: Runtime now stores the heavy-obscurement and light-shift prose as a sensory utility effect, but there is no typed obscurement state, 10-foot light-level transform, or self-attached darkness/dim-light area.
shadow-of-moil::vision_light_sound.recommendedTemplateChange: Add sensory/obscurement fields for self heavily obscured, light-level conversion radius, and affected light levels.

shadow-of-moil::healing_or_restoration.issue: The detector matched heavily obscured; this spell does not heal or restore anything.

shadow-of-moil::damage_reduction_or_prev.issue: Radiant resistance is now represented as a DEFENSIVE resistance effect.
shadow-of-moil::damage_reduction_or_prev.recommendedTemplateChange: Closed by adding Defense Type resistance.
shadow-of-moil::damage_reduction_or_prev.recommendedJsonChange: Closed by adding a DEFENSIVE Radiant resistance effect.

shadow-of-moil::conditional_ending.issue: The matched wording only says the effects last until the normal spell duration ends; there is no separate conditional ending.

sickening-radiance::choice_or_mode.issue: The detector matched choosing the center point of the area; this is ordinary area targeting, not a choice/mode mechanic.

sickening-radiance::attack_or_save_modif.issue: The Constitution save and Radiant damage are now represented for enter-area and turn-start triggers, but the same failed save also causes Exhaustion and a target light rider; runtime lacks shared-save rider semantics for those linked consequences.
sickening-radiance::attack_or_save_modif.recommendedTemplateChange: Add shared-save rider fields for damage plus exhaustion plus light emission.
sickening-radiance::attack_or_save_modif.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime sharedSaveRiders for Exhaustion and emitted light.

sickening-radiance::vision_light_sound.issue: The area Dim Light is partly represented, but failed-save targets emit Dim Light in a 5-foot radius and cannot benefit from Invisible; runtime lacks typed target light rider and anti-invisibility semantics.
sickening-radiance::vision_light_sound.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime targetLight and invisibleBenefitSuppression data.

sickening-radiance::ward_alarm_or_trigge.issue: The detector matched enter-area and turn-start hazard triggers, which are now represented as DAMAGE triggers rather than ward/alarm mechanics.
sickening-radiance::ward_alarm_or_trigge.recommendedJsonChange: Closed by on_enter_area and turn_start DAMAGE effects.

sickening-radiance::status_or_state_chan.issue: Failed-save creatures suffer one level of Exhaustion and cannot benefit from Invisible because of emitted light; runtime lacks typed Exhaustion application and invisible-benefit suppression.
sickening-radiance::status_or_state_chan.recommendedTemplateChange: Add Exhaustion level application fields and invisible-benefit suppression fields.
sickening-radiance::status_or_state_chan.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime status/rider data for Exhaustion level 1 and no Invisible benefit.

sickening-radiance::conditional_ending.issue: The spell explicitly removes the emitted light and any Exhaustion levels it caused when the spell ends, but runtime lacks typed cleanup of caused conditions/riders.
sickening-radiance::conditional_ending.recommendedTemplateChange: Add spell-end cleanup fields for caused conditions and light riders.

sickening-radiance::aftermath_or_memory.issue: The spell removes its light and Exhaustion at spell end, leaving no persistent aftermath.
sickening-radiance::aftermath_or_memory.recommendedTemplateChange: Track cleanup under conditional ending/status fields.

staggering-smite::attack_or_save_modifie.issue: The hit damage and attack-roll disadvantage rider are now represented, but ability-check disadvantage and the shared save that gates both non-damage riders are not fully expressible.
staggering-smite::attack_or_save_modifie.recommendedTemplateChange: Add ability-check disadvantage and shared-save rider fields.
staggering-smite::attack_or_save_modifie.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime abilityCheckModifier and sharedSaveRiders; current attack-roll modifier is a partial schema-safe encoding.

staggering-smite::reaction_or_opportunit.issue: On a failed Wisdom save the target can't take reactions until the end of its next turn; runtime lacks a typed reaction-lockout effect.
staggering-smite::reaction_or_opportunit.recommendedTemplateChange: Add reaction suppression fields with duration.

stone-shape::terrain_or_surface.issue: The detector matched passage-through-wall examples, but the spell reshapes stone objects or stone wall sections rather than creating terrain.
stone-shape::terrain_or_surface.recommendedTemplateChange: Track under created object/structure and object manipulation fields.

stone-shape::created_object_or_structure.issue: The spell reshapes a Medium-or-smaller stone object or a stone section no more than 5 feet in any dimension, can make passages or seal doors, and allows up to two hinges and a latch but no finer mechanical detail; runtime stores these manipulation limits only in prose.
stone-shape::created_object_or_structure.recommendedTemplateChange: Add object/structure manipulation fields for material, object size, section dimensions, allowed output examples, door sealing, hinge/latch limit, and fine-detail prohibition.

galders-speedy-courier::special_question.issue: Canonical prose is not_applicable for this 5etools-partial spell, so there is no trustworthy prose source for manual mechanics extraction in this pass.
galders-speedy-courier::special_question.recommendedTemplateChange: Decide whether partial-source spells need a canonical import/backfill bucket before schema closure.
galders-speedy-courier::special_question.recommendedJsonChange: Do not infer mechanics without canonical prose; runtime remains placeholder until a source is available.

freedom-of-movement::utility_type_runtim.issue: Structured markdown uses Utility Type movement, but runtime utilityType enum does not allow movement; using it caused JSON validation failure.
freedom-of-movement::utility_type_runtim.recommendedTemplateChange: Align structured Utility Type values with runtime utilityType enum or add movement to the runtime enum/schema.
freedom-of-movement::utility_type_runtim.recommendedJsonChange: Keep runtime utilityType as other until movement is schema-supported.

giant-insect::utility_type_runtime_gap.issue: Structured markdown uses Utility Type summoning, but runtime utilityType enum does not allow summoning; using it caused JSON validation failure.
giant-insect::utility_type_runtime_gap.recommendedTemplateChange: Align structured Utility Type values with runtime utilityType enum or add summoning to the runtime enum/schema.
giant-insect::utility_type_runtime_gap.recommendedJsonChange: Keep runtime utilityType as other until summoning is schema-supported.

mordenkainens-faithful-hound.canonicalEvidence: On your later turns, you can take a Magic action to move the hound up to 30 feet.
mordenkainens-faithful-hound.jsonState: Runtime JSON has no granted action or created-entity movement action.
mordenkainens-faithful-hound.issue: The caster can spend a Magic action on later turns to move the hound up to 30 feet, but runtime lacks this follow-up action.
mordenkainens-faithful-hound.recommendedTemplateChange: Add created-entity move action fields for action cost, distance, and controller.
mordenkainens-faithful-hound.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime grantedActions or createdEntityMovement data.
mordenkainens-faithful-hound.reviewerNote: Closed by adding Barrier Damage Block Directions and Barrier Damage Block Source Categories to structured markdown and effects[].barrierDamagePrevention in runtime JSON. The sphere's own all-damage immunity is also represented as a DEFENSIVE immunity effect. Disintegrate destruction remains open under the separate conditional_ending row.

mordenkainens-faithful-hound.canonicalEvidence: The hound remains for the duration or until the two of you are more than 300 feet apart from each other.
mordenkainens-faithful-hound.structuredState: Duration fields capture 8 hours, but no distance-ending field.
mordenkainens-faithful-hound.issue: Closed: The hound's early ending when caster and hound are more than 300 feet apart is now structured and runtime-mapped.
mordenkainens-faithful-hound.recommendedTemplateChange: Closed by Conditional Ending Triggers beyond_max_distance plus Conditional Ending Distance Feet 300.
mordenkainens-faithful-hound.recommendedJsonChange: Closed by runtime conditionalEndings data with trigger beyond_max_distance, spell scope, and distanceFeet 300.
mordenkainens-faithful-hound.closedReason: Mordenkainen's Faithful Hound now records the 300-foot caster/hound separation ending in structured markdown and runtime JSON. The hound's Magic action movement, invisibility/sound/truesight alarm behavior, and intangible/invulnerable created-entity semantics remain open in their own buckets.

mordenkainens-faithful-hound.canonicalEvidence: No one but you can see the hound, and it is intangible and invulnerable.
mordenkainens-faithful-hound.jsonState: Runtime JSON has no created-entity durability fields.
mordenkainens-faithful-hound.issue: The hound is intangible and invulnerable, but runtime lacks typed durability and physicality semantics.
mordenkainens-faithful-hound.recommendedTemplateChange: Add created-entity physicality and invulnerability fields.
mordenkainens-faithful-hound.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime createdEntity.physicality and invulnerable data.

mordenkainens-private-sanctum.canonicalEvidence: Canonical snapshot says the caster chooses security properties, but the properties themselves are omitted from the canonical comment while the structured description lists them.
mordenkainens-private-sanctum.structuredState: Structured Description includes sound blocking, vision blocking, divination sensor blocking, divination targeting blocking, teleport blocking, planar travel blocking, and 365-day permanence.
mordenkainens-private-sanctum.jsonState: Runtime JSON only keeps the lead-in sentence and omits the option list.
mordenkainens-private-sanctum.issue: The source layers disagree: the canonical snapshot is incomplete, but the structured markdown contains the missing ward options. This needs a backfill or source-trust decision before schema closure.
mordenkainens-private-sanctum.recommendedTemplateChange: Backfill the canonical snapshot or explicitly bless the structured property list as trusted source data for this spell.
mordenkainens-private-sanctum.recommendedJsonChange: After source backfill/approval, add typed warding options for sound, vision, divination, teleportation, planar travel, and permanence.

mordenkainens-private-sanctum.canonicalEvidence: When you cast the spell, you decide what sort of security the spell provides, choosing any of the following properties.
mordenkainens-private-sanctum.structuredState: Structured description lists six ward properties.
mordenkainens-private-sanctum.issue: The spell is a choice-of-ward-properties spell, but runtime has no typed property options.
mordenkainens-private-sanctum.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime controlOptions or wardOptions after source backfill.

mordenkainens-private-sanctum.canonicalEvidence: Casting this spell on the same spot every day for 365 days makes the spell last until dispelled.
mordenkainens-private-sanctum.jsonState: Runtime duration stays 24 hours with no permanence condition.
mordenkainens-private-sanctum.issue: Closed: Mordenkainen's Private Sanctum now records its 365 daily same-spot casts to until-dispelled progression.
mordenkainens-private-sanctum.recommendedTemplateChange: Closed by Duration Progression fields for repeated_casts, 365 daily same-location casts, and until_dispelled outcome.
mordenkainens-private-sanctum.closedReason: Mordenkainen's Private Sanctum now records the repeated daily same-spot casting rule in structured markdown and runtime JSON.

otilukes-resilient-sphere.canonicalEvidence: A shimmering sphere encloses a Large or smaller creature or object within range.
otilukes-resilient-sphere.structuredState: Valid Targets includes creatures and objects, but no size limit.
otilukes-resilient-sphere.jsonState: Runtime validTargets includes creatures and objects, but no size limit.
otilukes-resilient-sphere.issue: The target must be Large or smaller, and runtime lacks this size filter.
otilukes-resilient-sphere.recommendedTemplateChange: Add target size eligibility fields for creature/object targets.
otilukes-resilient-sphere.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime target size filter for Large or smaller.

otilukes-resilient-sphere.canonicalEvidence: A creature or object inside can't be damaged by attacks or effects originating from outside, nor can a creature inside the sphere damage anything outside it.
otilukes-resilient-sphere.jsonState: Runtime JSON stores damage prevention only in prose.
otilukes-resilient-sphere.issue: The sphere prevents inside/outside damage across the barrier, but runtime lacks typed damage-origin blocking.
otilukes-resilient-sphere.recommendedTemplateChange: Add barrier damage-prevention fields with origin side.
otilukes-resilient-sphere.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime barrier damage prevention data.
otilukes-resilient-sphere.reviewerNote: Closed by adding Barrier Damage Block Directions and Barrier Damage Block Source Categories to structured markdown and effects[].barrierDamagePrevention in runtime JSON. The sphere's own all-damage immunity is also represented as a DEFENSIVE immunity effect. Disintegrate destruction remains open under the separate conditional_ending row.

otilukes-resilient-sphere.canonicalEvidence: A Disintegrate spell targeting the globe destroys it without harming anything inside.
otilukes-resilient-sphere.jsonState: Runtime JSON has no spell-specific destruction condition.
otilukes-resilient-sphere.issue: Closed: Disintegrate targeting the globe now has a spell-scoped conditional ending without harming occupants.
otilukes-resilient-sphere.recommendedTemplateChange: Closed by Conditional Ending Triggers disintegrate_targets_effect with spell scope.
otilukes-resilient-sphere.recommendedJsonChange: Closed by runtime conditionalEndings data on the sphere utility and defensive effects.
otilukes-resilient-sphere.closedReason: Otiluke's Resilient Sphere now records the Disintegrate-targets-globe destruction rule in structured markdown and runtime JSON. The already closed barrier damage-prevention row continues to own the inside/outside damage blocking semantics.

phantasmal-killer.canonicalEvidence: On a failed save, the target takes 4d10 Psychic damage and has Disadvantage on ability checks and attack rolls for the duration.
phantasmal-killer.structuredState: No structured field for disadvantage on ability checks or attack rolls.
phantasmal-killer.jsonState: Runtime JSON has no ability check modifier and no attack roll modifier for this spell.
phantasmal-killer.issue: The failed-save rider is a nonstandard debuff, not a named condition, and runtime does not model it.
phantasmal-killer.recommendedTemplateChange: Add generic d20-test disadvantage rider fields.
phantasmal-killer.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime abilityCheckModifier/attackRollModifier shared-save rider data.

polymorph.canonicalEvidence: You attempt to transform a creature that you can see within range into a Beast.
polymorph.jsonState: Runtime utilityType remains other because transformation is not accepted by the current enum.
polymorph.issue: Structured markdown uses Utility Type transformation, but runtime utilityType enum does not allow transformation.
polymorph.recommendedTemplateChange: Align structured Utility Type values with runtime utilityType enum or add transformation to the runtime enum/schema.
polymorph.recommendedJsonChange: Keep runtime utilityType as other until transformation is schema-supported.

shadow-of-moil.canonicalEvidence: Whenever a creature within 10 feet of you hits you with an attack, the shadows lash out at that creature, dealing it 2d8 necrotic damage.
shadow-of-moil.structuredState: Effect Type now includes DAMAGE; Damage Dice: 2d8; Damage Type: Necrotic.
shadow-of-moil.jsonState: Runtime now has an on_attack_hit DAMAGE effect.
shadow-of-moil.issue: Retaliatory hit-trigger damage was hidden in prose and is now represented, though the 10-foot attacker range remains prose in the effect description.
shadow-of-moil.recommendedTemplateChange: No new damage field required for this pass; later attacker-range filters would improve precision.
shadow-of-moil.recommendedJsonChange: Closed by adding an on_attack_hit DAMAGE effect.

sickening-radiance.canonicalEvidence: Dim, greenish light spreads within a 30-foot-radius sphere centered on a point you choose within range.
sickening-radiance.structuredState: Targeting Type: area; Area Shape: sphere; Area Size: 30.
sickening-radiance.jsonState: Runtime targeting now uses area with a 30-foot Sphere.
sickening-radiance.issue: Runtime targeting previously used single despite carrying an areaOfEffect; this shape drift is now corrected.
sickening-radiance.recommendedJsonChange: Closed by changing targeting.type to area.

staggering-smite.canonicalEvidence: The next time you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack during this spell's duration, your weapon pierces both body and mind, and the attack deals an extra 4d6 psychic damage to the target.
staggering-smite.structuredState: Effect Type now includes DAMAGE; Damage Dice: 4d6; Damage Type: Psychic.
staggering-smite.jsonState: Runtime now has an on_attack_hit DAMAGE effect with first_hit consumption and melee weapon filter.
staggering-smite.issue: The smite damage rider was hidden in prose and is now represented as first-hit melee weapon damage.
staggering-smite.recommendedTemplateChange: No new damage-rider field required for this pass.
staggering-smite.recommendedJsonChange: Closed by adding the on_attack_hit DAMAGE effect.
